message:
fix(core): stabilize conformance oracle ORDER BY and add regression probes

Several conformance oracle queries produced non-deterministic row order
when the ORDER BY clause did not fully distinguish ties, causing
intermittent test failures when FrankenSQLite and rusqlite happened to
break ties differently.

Changes:
- s162: Add secondary `product` sort to the GROUP BY + ORDER BY
  COUNT(*) DESC query so tie-breaking is deterministic.
- s55d: Add secondary `m.name` sort to the self-join GROUP BY reports
  query for the same reason.
- s70d: Remove the dead Unicode-minus setup block that could never
  parse correctly; keep only the ASCII-minus setup_fixed block.
- s70q: Add new ORDER BY text regression probe that verifies basic
  text sorting (ASC, DESC, with WHERE filter) against the rusqlite
  oracle.
- s70r: Add new UPDATE CASE + ORDER BY regression probe that
  specifically targets the s69bc ORDER BY text regression observed
  after UPDATE with CASE expressions, ensuring post-mutation query
  results match the oracle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
